---
pagetitle: "Behaverse Data Model"
title-prefix: ""
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.bdm-hero}
:::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
[Behaverse · Data Model]{.kicker}

# All cognitive science data, in one coherent framework.

::: {.lead}
BDM defines how to organize, name, and describe experimental data—behavior,
questionnaires, physiological signals, and more—so datasets are consistent
across studies, clear to humans, and readily usable by machines.
:::
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
```{=html}
<div class="bdm-filetree">my-study/<br>├─ dataset.yaml<br>├─ events/<br>├─ trials/<br>│&nbsp;&nbsp;├─ response.csv<br>│&nbsp;&nbsp;└─ stimulus.csv<br>└─ models/</div>
```
:::
::::
:::

```{=html}
<nav class="bdm-routes" aria-label="Main sections">
  <a href="why/index.html"><span class="rn">01</span><br><span class="rt">Why BDM</span><br><span class="rd">the problem &amp; the payoff</span></a>
  <a href="concepts/index.html"><span class="rn">02</span><br><span class="rt">Concepts</span><br><span class="rd">the big picture in 15 minutes</span></a>
  <a href="guides/index.html"><span class="rn">03</span><br><span class="rt">Guides</span><br><span class="rd">your data into BDM</span></a>
  <a href="spec/index.html"><span class="rn">04</span><br><span class="rt">Specification</span><br><span class="rd">every table, field, and rule</span></a>
</nav>
```

Every lab organizes behavioral data its own way. The result: datasets that
cannot be combined, tools that cannot be reused, analyses that start from
scratch every time.[^1] And the data itself is harder than it looks — a key
press means nothing without the task, the stimulus, and the person behind it.

[^1]: Hulsebos et al. (2024), [*It took longer than I was
expecting*](https://doi.org/10.1145/3665939.3665959), on why dataset search
is still so hard.

BDM keeps three kinds of information distinct and linked: what was
**recorded** (events, signals), what it **means** given the task (trials,
derived by explicit rules), and what it says about **the agent** (models).
The result is a dataset whose meaning travels with it.

Datasets that share this structure can be read without emailing their
authors, joined across studies, checked automatically, and analyzed by tools
that were not written for them.

![The data journey: from experiment to a coherent, analyzable
dataset.](assets/img/data-journey.svg){fig-alt="Diagram: an experiment
produces events and signals (records); explicit task-patterns turn records
into trials (interpretations); models of agents (inferences) are fit on
either; everything lands in one consistently structured BDM dataset folder."}

::: {.bdm-proof}
[Read the paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2012.12583) (*The structure of
behavioral data*, arXiv 2020) · Builds on [tidy
data](https://www.jstatsoft.org/article/view/v059i10),
[xAPI](https://xapi.com/), [BIDS](https://bids.neuroimaging.io/),
[schema.org](https://schema.org), [CSVW](https://www.w3.org/TR/tabular-data-primer/)
· Developed openly [on GitHub](https://github.com/behaverse/data-model) ·
[How to cite](why/index.qmd#the-paper)
:::
